Extension prototype false positives: DATA-001/SEC-080 are the documented hardcoded demo vault (demo emails + placeholder passwords) in extension/popup.js — README states production uses a native-messaging bridge and never bundles secrets; SEC-163 matches are CSS selector strings in content.js (input[autocomplete=current-password], querySelector), not secrets.

Web-vault false positives: app/src/lib/seed.ts is the documented first-run DEMO vault (demo emails, placeholder passwords, demo TOTP secrets) — sealed by real crypto on first unlock, nothing special-cased; AddItemDialog.vue:80 is an email input placeholder; ItemDetail.vue:96 renders the user's own decrypted password on reveal (login.password), not a hardcoded secret.

Accepted+tracked: gtk-rs 0.18 / unic / proc-macro-error advisory set (RUSTSEC-2024-0411..0420, 0370, 0429; 2025-0075..0100; GHSA-wrw7-89jp-8q8g) pulled ONLY transitively by Tauri v2's Linux GTK GUI backend, in the DETACHED app/src-tauri desktop shell (its own workspace, not built by the core product/server). All are unmaintained/unsound advisories, not remote-exploitable; not on the macOS runtime path (WebKit backend). No core crate (passbook/sync/crypto/broker) or the sync-server is affected. Revisit when Tauri bumps its gtk-rs pins.

All in the isolated demo/ environment (never shipped to production): seed.sh uses example.com demo emails; docker-compose KEYWARD_SYNC_ADDR 0.0.0.0 is bind-all (not a public IP); the demo Dockerfile's rust/debian base images are unpinned — digest-pinning is left to a real deployment to keep the throwaway demo simple.

deploy/ k8s template — intentional design for the self-host / paid-managed-cloud target (v1.31.0). Single replica + Recreate strategy are REQUIRED by the file-backed store on a ReadWriteOnce PVC (documented in deploy/README.md). Ingress TLS IS present via cert-manager. NetworkPolicy/HA/PodDisruptionBudget deliberately deferred (single stateful replica). Base images pinned to tags (rust:1.90-bookworm, debian:bookworm-slim) — acceptable for the OSS template; pin to digests at managed-cloud release. IAC-464 namespace findings are informational. Revisit these at managed-cloud GA.

Single replica + Recreate strategy are REQUIRED by the file-backed store on a ReadWriteOnce PVC (documented in deploy/README.md). Ingress TLS IS present via cert-manager. NetworkPolicy/HA/PodDisruptionBudget deliberately deferred (single stateful replica). Base images pinned to tags (rust:1.90-bookworm, debian:bookworm-slim) — acceptable for the OSS template; pin to digests at managed-cloud release. IAC-464 namespace findings are informational. Revisit these at managed-cloud GA.

False positive: deploy/Dockerfile sets KEYWARD_SYNC_ADDR=0.0.0.0:8787 — 0.0.0.0 is the intended bind-all address for a containerized server (behind the k8s ingress), not a hardcoded public IP or PII.

False positive: FamilyDetail.vue renders the selected shared login's password in the vault UI (masked, with a Show toggle) — this is displaying a user's own vault field, not a hardcoded credential/secret.

deploy/docker-compose.pg.yml is a LOCAL-DEV convenience file: throwaway 'keyward:keyward' Postgres creds guard an ephemeral local container with no real data — not a production secret (nothing to rotate). Production runs from deploy/k8s against a managed DB with KEYWARD_SYNC_PG injected from a k8s Secret (documented in the file header + ADR-0005). The 0.0.0.0:8787 is the intended container bind address, not a public IP.

False positive: the scanner is matching a snippet inside its own .nox/baseline.json (a previously-recorded finding's context), not live code or config.
False positive: the scanner is matching the example email inside its own .nox/baseline.json (a prior baselined finding's recorded snippet), not live code.

deploy/k8s posture — intentional design / informational for the managed-cloud template (v1.34.0). The app tier is stateless + HA (2 replicas, RollingUpdate, HPA 2→10 @70% CPU — sane thresholds). Postgres is a bundled single-replica StatefulSet by design (DB single-writer; production uses a managed DB / operator for HA, documented in deploy/README.md). Secrets are referenced via secretKeyRef env (standard k8s pattern; the app never logs them). NetworkPolicy deferred; namespace/PDB/HPA findings are informational. fsGroup on Postgres is intentional for volume ownership. Revisit NetworkPolicy + PDB at managed-cloud GA.

False positive: deploy/README.md shows `--from-literal=postgres-password="$PG_PASSWORD"` — a documented shell VARIABLE reference for creating the Secret out-of-band, not a hardcoded password.

GA-hardening manifests (v1.36.0), all informational. IAC-395 is a POSITIVE finding — it fires because we now ship a PodDisruptionBudget; minAvailable:1 against replicas:2 (HPA 2→10) is intentional and correct, and Postgres is deliberately left without a PDB so node drains aren't blocked. IAC-464 ×2 are the standard "namespace defined" informationals for the new networkpolicy.yaml / pdb.yaml.

False positive: deploy/k8s/deployment.yaml:50 is the wait-for-postgres initContainer's DIGEST-PINNED image reference (postgres:16.4-alpine@sha256:5660c2...). The 64-char hex digest matches the SEC-680 API-key pattern. This is supply-chain hardening (the very thing CONT-001 asks for), not a credential — there is nothing to rotate, and the same digest is pinned in postgres.yaml.
